mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for Cluster API to function, as they define the custom resources that will be managed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This installs the CRDs necessary for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace, enabling the operator to manage clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This installs the Cluster API components in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Cluster API cluster, which is the primary resource managed by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This removes the Cluster API Helm release.
> This deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your existing clusters. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > clusters-backup.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup existing clusters.
> This upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs are correctly applied using the provided YAML file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the Cluster API controller is not responding, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation for the Cluster API pods and ensure they have sufficient CPU and memory.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if there are misconfigurations in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the affected pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster resource.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3' for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your Cluster resources to the new API version 'v1alpha4'.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your created clusters with their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher with kubectl and Helm installed.
